title: NCPDP Rx Reliance Companion Profile
summary: A portable evidence sidecar that rides beside an existing NCPDP transaction (bound by an action digest) and lets every party compute the SAME reliance verdict before approving, denying, dispensing, transferring, or auditing a drug transaction.

verdict_semantics.rx_rely: The determination is admissible to rely on. Covers BOTH a dispensable APPROVE (all evidence composes) and a signed, reasoned, appeal-ready DENY. Reliance is not approval; result.determination says which.
verdict_semantics.fail_closed: Any required leg that is absent, unverifiable, mis-bound, stale, or from an unpinned issuer yields the matching rx_do_not_rely_* verdict, never a pass.

prescriber_authority.carrier: EP-AUTHORITY-PROOF-v1 (scoped to the drug action), subject-bound to the Class-A prescriber signoff
prescriber_authority.verified_by: the composed EP-RELIANCE-KERNEL (authority scope/limit/validity/revocation, subject binding)

patient_consent.carrier: EP-RX-CONSENT-v1 (Ed25519-signed, carries a pairwise patient reference and keyed consent commitment, no source record), bound to the action digest

clinical_evidence.carrier: EP-RX-CLINICAL-v1 (Ed25519-signed diagnosis/lab attestation, carries a keyed evidence commitment and coded criterion, no source record), bound to the action digest

benefit_policy.carrier: the request's policy_hash pinned to the payer formulary policy; an RTBP benefit_check with checked_at and policy_hash
benefit_policy.verified_by: the composed kernel (policy pin) plus an Rx freshness gate on the RTBP benefit check

revocation_freshness.carrier: a revocation-currency check on the prescriber authority
revocation_freshness.note: a stale or revoked authority reads as rx_do_not_rely_missing_prescriber_authority

signed_denial.carrier: EP-RX-DENIAL-v1 (Ed25519-signed reason code + keyed reason commitment + generic appeal route), bound to the action digest
signed_denial.verified_by: verifyRxArtifact under an accepted_payer_keys pin, required only when determination is 'deny'

acceptance_discipline: VERIFIED (signature and digest checks pass) is kept separate from ACCEPTED (issuer key pinned by the relying party). The relying party pins its own keys and policy; EMILIA authors no bar and is never in the trust path.
privacy_profile: EP-NCPDP-RX-PRIVACY-PROFILE-v1. Signed artifacts have exact allowlists, patient references are pairwise, and source records use keyed commitments. This is a minimization control, not a legal-compliance claim.
exports: EP-RX-RELIANCE-BUNDLE-v2 — a content-addressed, PHI-minimized projection with keyed references to evidence retained in the holder's controlled vault.
conformance: conformance/vectors/ncpdp-rx-reliance.v1.json (positive approve + positive signed deny + a reject for every rx_do_not_rely_* verdict); tests/ncpdp-rx-reliance.test.ts.
wedge: CMS-0057-F pushes medical prior auth through FHIR APIs but repeatedly excludes drugs from those requirements, leaving drug PA / pharmacy benefit authorization as the NCPDP-owned gap this profile targets.
standards_status: Candidate companion profile. No EP Internet-Draft is IETF-adopted or endorsed; this profile is not an NCPDP standard or operating rule until NCPDP adopts it.
